mission.title: backstage: [RFC] Standardize AWS authentication in Backstage
mission.description: [RFC] Standardize AWS authentication in Backstage. Requested by 36+ users.

steps.title: Check current Backstage deployment and version
steps.description:
Verify your Backstage version supports the unified AWS credentials provider. Check your `package.json` for the Backstage version:
```bash
cat package.json | grep '@backstage/core'
```
This feature requires Backstage v1.10+ with the `@backstage/integration-aws-node` package.

steps.title: Configure unified AWS authentication in app-config.yaml
steps.description:
Add the standardized AWS integration configuration to your `app-config.yaml`. This provides a common credential provider for all AWS-dependent Backstage plugins:
```yaml
integrations:
  aws:
    default:
      # Optional: falls back to default credential provider chain
      accessKeyId: ${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID}
      secretAccessKey: ${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}
      assumeRole: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/backstage-main-role
      externalId: 'some-id'
    accounts:
      # Optional: if no accounts are specified, falls back to default creds
      - accountId: '111111111111'
        assumeRole: arn:aws:iam::111111111111:role/backstage-role
        externalId: 'hello'
      - accountId: '222222222222'
        assumeRole: arn:aws:iam::222222222222:role/backstage-role
        externalId: 'world'
```
This replaces per-plugin credential configuration with a unified approach.

steps.title: Install the AWS integration package
steps.description:
Install the unified AWS credential provider package:
```bash
yarn --cwd packages/backend add @backstage/integration-aws-node
```
Backend plugins that need AWS access (S3 discovery, TechDocs with S3 storage, etc.) will automatically use the credentials defined in the integration configuration.

steps.title: Verify AWS authentication works
steps.description:
Start Backstage and confirm that AWS-dependent features work correctly:
```bash
yarn dev
```
Test by accessing features that require AWS credentials (e.g., S3-based catalog locations, TechDocs stored in S3). Check the Backstage logs for any AWS authentication errors.

resolution.summary: A unified AWS credential provider library (@backstage/integration-aws-node) centralizes AWS authentication configuration in app-config.yaml, replacing per-plugin credential setup. Supports default credentials, per-account role assumption, and optional per-plugin overrides.

      - accountId: '222222222222'
        assumeRole: arn:aws:iam::222222222222:role/backstage-role
        externalId: 'world'

prerequisites.description: A running Backstage instance with backend plugins that require AWS access (e.g., S3 catalog locations, TechDocs).
